Ice_Log: 8PM to Mid: Passed pieces of floating ice.
Mid to 4AM: One large berg in sight at close of watch to Wd. Merid to 4PM: One large berg sighted ahead at end of watch.
4AM to 8AM: Passed a large ice berg at 5:30am
11:45: Several icebergs in sight, one about 250ft long and 40ft high.
4PM to 8PM: Passed a large ice berg at 5:45pm.
7:30AM: Steamed through moderate sized field of water washed ice.
Merid to 4PM: Fields of ice on each bow. and ahead. Coursing ? ship through loose ice during watch several bergs in sight.
8:30AM: Made out ice ahead and on both bows.
9:00am: Entered a field of pack ice. Steering through pack ice for the rest of the watch.

8PM to Mid: Steaming through pack ice a few bergs in sight changed course at short intervals to avoid large pieces. 8PM: To NxE Passed occasional small pieces Humps of ice very open pack scattered bergs visible  to Ewd.
Merid to 4PM: Several icebergs in sight.
2:00AM: Passed through quite a thick pack of ice.. Ice in sight all around horizen.
9:30am: Passed through a couple of strings of very loose heavy ice, no more ice in sight during watch.
8PM to Mid: Several small bergs in sight during watch.
Mid to 4AM: Passed numerous pieces of detatched ice. Merid to 4PM: Stearing general course NE1/4N through pack ice & bergs. A very large number of icebergs around ship during watch.
4AM to 8AM: Passed several strings of ice: several icebergs in sight.
8AM to Noon: Steaming to North approximatly parallel to coast in sight along stbd beam through heavy pack ice changing course to avoid heavy floes.
10:00am: Steaming through pack ice. Numerous bergs in sight ahead at close of watch.
Note: Estimating course and distance 10 to noon incl.

Ice_Log: 4PM to 8PM: Steering through loose ice for harbor of Godhavn.

Note: Estimating course and distance to midnight.
Steaming for Godhavn harbor along pack ice.

Ice_Log: Merid to 4PM: Experimented with 2.8lbs of guncotton torpedoes, 1st placed in hole in ice resulted in placing several cracks in ice varying from 10 to 350 ft in length and starting several sheets of ice. 2nd placed on top of ice had only local effect and no cracks. Mid to 4AM: Steaming through pack ice along coast for Godhavn Harbor.
Note: Position 5 miles  W Mag  of Sternum

Ice_Log: Steaming through pack ice for Godhavn Harbor. 2:05am: Entered lead steering along coast to Ed. Ice in solid pack except for this lead.
7:30am: Steamed up to the ice in the mouth of the harbor of Godhavn and made ship fast.

8AM to Noon: Laying alongside ice in Godhavn harbor. Experimented with 8lb gunpowder torpedo in blowing up ice in harbor for docking ship.   Explosion made only a small hole 8ft in diameter purely local effect.
4PM to 8PM: Steaming along shore of Disco Is, through large sheets of floating ice. At 8:00pm opposite Disco Fjord.
8PM to Mid: Much loose pack ice and scattered bergs to seaward. Clear water along land.
Mid to 4AM: Steamed through leads in the ice for NE end of Hare Island.
2:45am: Stopped engines having arrived at solid pack ice starting from shore at the Nd a few water shicks (?) near NE end of Hare Island being the only open waterin sight.
3:30am: The ice appering solid all the way across & ahead & no chance to proceed headed ship to Sd. Steering ship back towards Godhavn last half hour of watch.  .

Note: His courses and speeds from here to Godhavn are way off. Ignored them.

Ice_Log: 5:00pm: Arrived at edge of ice in outer anchorage off Godhavn and made fast to edge of ice.  .

Note: mid: Opposite northern end of Disco Is.

Ice_Log: 8:50: Ice began to break up necessitating continued shifting the hold of our ice anchor.

Note: 2:10 underway steaming to north about 2 1/2 miles from land.

Ice_Log: 4PM to 8PM: some streeks of loose ice to seawards. Clear water from six to seven miles off shore. Occassional moderate sized bergs.

Note: To N of entrance to Disko Fjord

Ice_Log: 10:15pm: Entrance to Weigat Channel appeared clear of ice. Ice thick to Nd & Wd clear lead along th eland & close to hare Is.
0000: Stearing through ice towards hare Is.
2:25am: At end of lead made fast to ice hummock to await opening.
4:00am: Laying fast to floe 3 miles off south end of Hare Is.
5:30: Ice opened, forsed way through barrier into open water.

11:30am: Ice extending to horizon in every direction ahead but much broken up.  A few streaks of water in it, a few bergs, ice about the ship one winters ice. much broken & apparently out of Weigalt (?) Straits.

2:45pm: The ice having moved somewhat showing a lead to Nd & Ed cast off from floes and steamed into lead. Lead open at times & again filled with thick ice pans & broken ice.  Continued along the lead during remainder of watch at times ramming a passage through thick ice pans & drift ice.
4PM to 8PM: Steaming through water lanes in ice, in a generally northerly direction.
5:00pm: Made fast to floe.
8PM to Mid: Ice opening cast off moorings & at 11:00pm started ahead through loose pack of much broken ice. A few moderate sized bergs in pack.
Mid to 4AM: Steaming through leads in the ice. The ice pans very thick & heavy. Making very little progress.   2:45am stopped and moored to floe. A steam whaler in shore of us fast to the ice.
5:30am: Ice slacked a little worked our way through ice towards land 3 miles.
7:00: Ice closed & stopped our passage.
4:15pm: Leads opened to the Ed steamed through leads between packs & through pack ice working in towards land.
7:30pm: Made the land about 3 miles dist.

Note: 11:30: Settlement of Noureask (?) bearing SxW by standard compass.

Ice_Log: Mid to 4AM: Steaming slowely through open lead into shore.  At close of watch in open streak of about mile wide along shore steaming Nd along coast to Nourwak Peninsula (?) 1/2 mile dist .
2:30 Steamed into open lead in to shore.

4AM to 8AM: Steaming along the coast 1/4 mile off between Naurasoak & Kamasak points we passed close to two large bergs aground 150 yds from shore and came to a tight bar of ice & made fast to land ice.
Steering through ice to Nd & Ed
8AM to Noon: Ice moving slightly to the Wd.
1:45pm: Leads showing to Nd, working through broken leads until 2:25pm when leads closed up made fast to floe.
Steaming to Nth through pack ice. 8PM to Mid: Steaming through leads in the ice & through broken ice.
Note: Kanissut Point brg SE1/2E Mag 3 miles

Ice_Log: 9:30pm: Could no longer make any way the ice being in large solid pans:    The ice appeared nuch packed the edges piles high. The blocks showing evidence of former sever nips. The water in the leads covered with slab ice from one to two inches in thickness. Large number of icebergs in sight.
Mid to 4AM: The ice still clear in the lead near the ship. Strong tide is observed running to SW.
4AM to 8AM: Steaming through Omanak Fiord through bays of water and black slop. Passed several large bergs, one about 20yds distant.
8AM to Noon: Steaming through ice pack to Nd & Ed. Ice in large unbroken hummocky sheets covered with 2ft of snow.  Water pools covered with soft sludge ice several inches thick.
11:00am: No more leads open to Nd & Ed, made fast to ice.
12:25am: Underway through new snow ice to Nd. By working through loose ice reached clear water at 4:00am. Set course Nd.

Mid to 4AM: Tied to ice alll watch.  Loose ice driving to Sd before the wind.   At close of watch no ice except scattered bergs visible from deck from SSW to NNW.  Open water visible to the Nd but seperated from the ship by almost two miles of fixed ice held in line with grounded berg off Savaster (?) Nook.
2:07: The ice opened a little and got underway. Steamed into open lead to Nd. At 2:25 came to mooring to the ice there being no open lead.
8:35am: Underway and steamed about 3 miles to Nd, came to at 9:35 alongside a floe.
4PM to 8PM: ice still tight.

Mid to 4AM: Tied fast to ice all watch.  Sea claer of ice from SSW to NW by N.  Ice jammed  to Nd of vessel. Merid to 4PM: At 12:30 underway working through loose and new ice.  at 4pm got clear of ice strings in sight.
4AM to 8AM: Ice braking away ahead. At 4:30 cast off and worked around the sheets & bergs and steamed to NE.

Note: 7:00am: Black Hook abeam about 1 league dist

Ice_Log: 8AM to Noon: Clear water till 9:30am.   At 10:00am encountered loose ice in leads between large floes and pans.  The remainder of the watch ramming through the loose ice which in places was held by new ice one to one half inches in thickness.   At 12 the flow closed preventing further advance of the ship.  Flows moving and grinding together. .
4PM to 8PM: Occasional strings of loose ice, many bergs.  At close of watch clear water, no ice in sight.
8PM to Mid: Occasional streams of loose ice visible during last hour of watch and ice bergs passed occasionally.
Merid to 4PM: Sent oficer to obtain ice for fresh aater from large berg near ship. Mid to 4AM: !Cornwallis! got underway to Ed along edge of ice.
2:30am: Ice close and heavy to Nd & showing no lead. large berg just ahead of ship.
4AM to 8AM: Ice tight to the Nd & Ed no chance of getting further at present.
4PM to 8PM: Underway forcing ship through one narrow lead heading SWxS. Many bergs & loose strings of ice in sight.

Note: 10:40pm: Made fast to ice off settlement of Upernavik.

Ice_Log: 4am to 8am: Ice breaking up in our berth.
Mid to 4AM: ship fast to ice. Merid to 4PM: Tied to ice all watch.  Ice pilot reported all the whalers in sight a few miles to Nd & the ice unbroken as far as could be seen with long glass.  .

Note: Noon: Moored alongside of ice floe opposite settlement of Kingatok.

Ice_Log: Mid to 8AM: laying fast to ice.

Note: 8AM to Noon: cast off & steamed around Kingatok Island. At 10:30am made fast to ice on western side of island.

Ice_Log: Noon to 4PM: Steaming through leads & broken ice following the lead of the !Thetis!.
2:15pm: Stopped and made fast to floe. 2:30pm cast off from floe.
Note: 2:00 passed just E'd of Berry Island.
3:30 Berry Island NNW, nearest point of mainland S1/2E. Grounded at 3:15, off at 3:30.

Ice_Log: 4PM to 8PM: Steaming through ice. at 5:00pm made fast to ice floe.

Note: 8PM to Mid: 11:30am cast off and followed Thetis towards open water.

Ice_Log: 4AM to 8AM: Ice tight no water to be seen Nd. Light jam.
8AM to Noon: Ice showed no movement & no leads or open water in sight from Crows nest.
4AM to 8AM: Ship fast to ice.
10:12: Cast off from ice. Received intelligence that there was clear water to the Nd of Norse Head.

Note: 3:45pm: Made fast to floe next to!Thetis!

Ice_Log: 4PM to Mid: Moored to ice all watch. 4PM to 8PM: No visible water to Nd.  Native reported on open water beyong Cape Shackleton.
Mid to 4AM: Ice close to Nd, no leads.

Note: Moved him 10 minutes east to get off land.

Ice_Log: 6:40pm: cast off and steamed out of harbor, breaking way through ice in a general N direction.

8PM to Mid: Steaming slowely seaward through very narrow leads in wake of !Thetis!.  Ice about 3 1/2 ft thick in large unbroken sheets, many bergs. At 9:30pm !Thetis! caught in light nip. At 10:45pm moored to ice astern of !Thetis!.  Attempted to free Thetis with Gun cotton torpedoes and failed to loosen the ice.

Mid to 4AM: Ship anchored to ice floe. Large lead to Nd & Ed in plain sight but unable to enter it. Merid to 4PM: Steaming Nd in wake of !Thetis!, through narrow leads between large unbroken sheets. Several small bergs necessitated ramming by both ships. At close of watch a large open lead  off Cape Shackleton.
4AM to 8AM: Moored to ice first part of watch. at 6:55am !Thetis! backed out of nip. At 7:55 we followed.
8AM to Noon: !Thetis! engaged in forcing passage through ice. At 9:35 passage cleared. Remainder of watch steaming in general NE direction through leads in ice.

4PM to 8PM: Steaming through leads in the ice towards the Duck Islands. At 6:10 !Thetis made fast to ice floe, th elead having terminated. This vessle made fast to !Thetis!  The ice presented appearence of having broken into several leads now closed by wind and tide.
8PM to Mid: No movement in ice sufficent to open lead.
Note: 8:00pm: Made fast to flow 5 miles SW of Duck Islands

Ice_Log: Merid to 4PM: fast to ice awaiting leads about three miles SW of Duck Islands.
9:00am: cast of from ice.
10:35am: Arrived at check in the lead.
11:00am: Bear and Thetis ranning floe. Found floe solid and 30! thick, and at 12 stopped ramming having broken ice a couple of ships lengths. Made fast to ice.
9:30pm: Ramming at lead till 10:45. when having finished with the close ice, entered now open lead.
Mid to 4AM: Very small sheet of open water to Nd of islands and no leads in sight.
Note: 12:45 made fast SW of middle island.

Ice_Log: 4AM to 8AM: No change in ice.
8AM to Noon: Fast to ice.
12:45am: Made fast to ice SW of the middle island (Duck Islands).
4PM to 8PM: At end of watch no leads to the Nd in sight from crows nest.

Note: Mid to 4AM: Very small sheet of open water to Nd of islands and no leads in sight.

Ice_Log: 4AM to 8AM: No change in ice.
8AM to Noon: Fast to ice.
12:45am: Made fast tice SW of the middle island (Duck Islands).
4PM to 8PM: At end of watch no leads to the Nd in sight from crows nest.
Mid to 4AM: No change in condition of ice .
8AM to Noon: Much broken ice floating to and fro with the tide and wind.
8PM to Mid: Ice in broken parts moving to Sd & Wd in large quantity.
Mid to 4AM: Edd tide.  Large quantities of loose ice drifting to Sd. Merid to 4PM: The !Aurora! was seen at the end of the watch well to the Wd fast in the pack ice.
8AM to Noon: Fog lifted giving view of ice well off shore drifting to Sd.
6:00pm: Shifted quarter and bow lines farther in shore to solid ice, the edges of the floes breaking off.
Mid to 4AM: At close of watch steaming through leads to Nd. Mid to 4AM: Ice near ship softening and showing signs of breaking up.
Note: 0000: Sailing from Duck Islands to Cape York.

Ice_Log: 4AM to 8AM: The Aurora was seen at end of watch jammed in by large pans and floes and attempting to get to Sd.
5:45am: Followed Thetis into leads, making general course N by W
8:45am: No leads opening to the Nd made fast to ice.
Note: 8AM to Noon: Four steam whalers in sight to Nd fast in ice.

Ice_Log: 10:15am: Thetis attempted to force way through a close nip to an open lead and not succeding came to at 11:00am
2:00pm: Thetis ramming several times at the bar of ice but failed to break passage. .
3:15pm: Ice slacked showing lead into water to Nd. Cast off at 3:40.

4PM to 8PM: Steaming through a lead between floes first hour and in wake of Thetis to Nd & Ed. At 5:00pm arrived at edge of large floes completely stopping further progress. Made fast to floe.  At 6:30pm cast off steaming to Nd & Ed, making fast at 6:50pm.  The ice closing in the lead just left and moving in a general direction to Sd & Wd.  Open water visible from mast head to the Nd & Ed of us and extending parallel to the trend of the land.

8PM to Mid: Ice in sight to Sd but no leads and very little open water. Numerous bergs in sight.  At 9pm cast off steaming in general NE direction through small water leads and much broken ice. Stopped at 10:50 unable to forse way any further.
Mid to 8AM: Ice tight all about. Merid to 4PM: Lying made fast to ice.
8AM to Noon: The ice in the immediate vacinity of this ship broken up into large pans which were closely packed together.
3:15pm: The ice loosened to Nd & Ed. Cast off and got underway.    Remainder of watch steaming through short leads and through loose ice in general NE direction.

4PM to 8PM: Atric ahead of Thetis stopped by narrow bar of ice at 4:45 and failed to break it..  Made fast to the ice at 5:30pm to wait for it to slack streaks of open water visible to Nd. Ice slack to Sd & Nd. Ice 5ft thick.

8PM to Mid: The ice near the ship to the Nd broken up into large pans which with the tide were packing in Nd.  At 10:30 hauled ship astern to be clear of nip from close packing of ice pans.  The ice to Nd showed open leads at long intervals with spaces of close ice & packed pans between.
Note: 8pm: Sun shining on Greenland coast to Sd & Ed near Horsehead & Cape Shackleton.

Ice_Log: Mid to 4AM: Ice loose to S&Wd and small spaces of open water visible to Nd.
Note: mid: Devils Thumb bore at midnight E1/8N, Wilcop (?) Head E,

Ice_Log: 4PM to 8PM: The ice slacking got underway at 4:40pm and steamed in wake of Thetis through loose ice about 2 1/2 miles in general NE direction.  Not being able to force way through secured to ice floe at 6:20pm.  Ice loose but in many heavy pans from 4 to 6 ft thick.   .
8PM to Mid: Beset in th eice all watch. .

Mid to 4AM: 2:00 steaming through narrow leads, heavy loose ice, towards Nilcop (?) Head.  Progress slow owing to frequent stopping to turn sharp ?, or shore sheets out of way.  General course SE dist about 2 miles .
Note: mid: Devils Thumb bore at midnight E1/8N, Wilcop (?) Head E,

Ice_Log: 1AM: Devils Thumb bore E1/4N (psc) ships head NE by E.  The loose pans around the ship having a confused motion in all directions. Large floes came down pressing smaller pan against rudder. Ice in vacinity of ships in large pans pressed together and moving with the tide to Nd & Wd. Ships drifting with ice and tide.
Note: The position of WIlcox Head & Devils Thumb as laid down on the [blank] is about 15 miles to S'd of its true position.

Ice_Log: 4AM to 8AM: At 5:45am  Thetis attempted to force way through ice to open water. Cast off, closed distance from Thetis & secured to ice at 6:30pm having made about 12/3 of a mile NE.

8AM to Noon: Ice in small pans and loosening, moving with ebb tide.  At 10:30am got underway following Thetis.  Forced way through small ice jams and entered large pond of clear water and steamed to its southern edge, making fast to ice at 11:30am. there being no open water beyond.  Ice broken inshore, but not enough to admit passageof ships. Broken ice in sight to Nd as far as Red Head and another lead of broken ice extending in direction of Brown Islands. Made when underway about 2 miles in NNE direction.

4PM to 8PM: Steaming, making cource about NE by N at intervals passing through pan ice and part of the time in open leads.  The ice very thick in places. Th eice appears broken up into large pans in a space of several miles in width extending in NE direction.  Ice inshore appearing but little broken.
Note: Devil's Thumb SE1/4E, Duck Ids SW1/4W

Ice_Log: 8PM to Mid: Steaming through large water spaces and occasionally through close pans of broken ice. Ice heavy and in large pans.

Mid to 4AM: First two hours steaming full speed on a general NE course through wide apart lanes of water in wake of Thetis.  Passed through a couple narrow bars of loose broken ice.  At 2:10am made fast to ice, further progress to Nd being barred by solid ice.   To Ed ice unbroken, to Nd in large sheets; many bergs fast in it. Merid to 4PM: 2:35am got underway in wake of Thesis, began working way through loose ice and small water lanes in a general NNE direction and at end of watch made 2 miles.  At 3:45 landed watchon ice to attempt to relieve pressure on a tight jam by (blowing?) small pieces away. At end of watch still in ice.

Note: 2:10am: Bearings (pc) Cape Walker ENE3/4E, Cape Diddon SE by E1/2E

Ice_Log: 8AM to Noon: No change in ice conditions.

4PM to 8PM: At 6:40am the ice having opened, we cast off and folowed Thetis about 1 1/2 miles to NNE & made fast again at 7:10am. 4PM to 8PM: At 4:45 followed Artic, aurora and Thetis through narrow passage in the bar. Got in wide lanes of open water shortly after steaming full speed in a general NE by N direction. At close of watch following edge of fast ice in wide clear water lanes.

8PM to Mid: Steaming through leads and the first two hours an occasionan bar of broken ice. The bight of Melville Bay in plain sight.  Ice in large floes with a wide lead following the course of the coast.

Mid to 4AM: Steaming in general NW direction in open water.  At 2:55am having arrived at end of lead, made fast to ice.  Ice solid to north and west. No open water in sight. Merid to Mid: Lying fast in ice floe with no change in ice conditions.

8AM to Noon: The ice in vacinity of ship moving across the direction of the wind, and in large floes. The floes alternatily nipping and opening.  At 10:30am the large floes to which Thetis, Wolf and this ship were made fast split drifting to Nd. At 11:40am underway to large floe and made fast again .
Mid to 4AM: No chnage in position of ship of ice conditions.
4AM to 8AM: No change in ice.

8PM to Mid: 9:05pm cast off and followed Aurora, Wolf & Thetis through slack ice & narrow leads in a general NNE direction.  At 11am got into open water extending towards Cape York, in sight bearing NE by N.

Mid to 4AM: Under full steam through broad leads in the ice.  At 1:35 reached a bar of loose ice pans near large bergs. This bar was about four miles across.  At 2:00am the Aurora emerged from the pan ice into the North water, her crew giving three cheers as they steamed into it.  The Wolf and Theis and we followed her and shortly afterwards we passed the Thesis & wolf laying our course through open water. Mid to 4AM: The ice in large floes wide leads and water holes. At 1:15pm made fast to the ice. .
6am: Went ahead steering along the edge of the floe to find a lead to Nd.
Note: 6am: Ships head SSE, Cape York bearing N by E, dist about 4 miles.

Ice_Log: 8AM to Noon: Making wat through ? pans of ice and short leads in towards the shore.  At end of watch in large open water Nr lead to Nd in sight .

Note: Noon: By bearing Cape York. This is about six miles from the real location and his bearings and distance.

Ice_Log: 4PM to Mid: No change in ice.
Mid to 4AM: No leads to Nd open. Merid to 4PM: Ice tight to Nd. Fast to floe.

4AM to 8AM: Two floes came together astern of sdhip and rafted very heavily.   Aurora in a hard nip. Lowered all her boats and launched them clear of the ship and floe in case the ship should get smashed.
8AM to Noon: Ice conditions remained same.
10:00pm: Ice conditions about same as in previous watch. .

Merid to 4PM: Steaming through leads and loose ice. Much slab holding pans of hard ice. Ice much broken. Many ice bergs.  At 3pm passed close to four large walrus on a small pan of ice. Mid to 4AM: Ice appeared to be slacking and drifting to SE and last hour of watch to SW. Lying fast to ice floe awaiting leads to Nd.
4AM to 8AM: At 6:00am cast off and steamed into leads to NNE.  Steaming through wide open water with frequent patches of small broken ice.
8AM to Noon: Many bergs and much broken ice. Ice soft & only about two feet thick.

4PM to 8PM: steaming through loose and short leads in a general NE direction.  At 5:30pm stopped by a jam between two heavy pans, & after attempting to force way through by ramming gave it up as the floes were comming together and shifting to a large floe some 1/2 mile to Sd. Came to at 6:55pm.  At end of watch lying fast to ice.

Note: 11:30pm: East end of Wolstenholme Island SE by E, West end to E1/2S. Cape Athol SSE3/4E

Ice_Log: Mid to 4AM: At 12 got underway out through loose pans and open leads to Nd.  Shaped course for cary Is. Numbers of ice bergs in sight. The ice pans greatly increasing in thickness and by 2:00am ? encountered ?hy heavy ice though small in extent.   After 2:00am steaming various courses keeping out of the pack ice which was found very thick. . Merid to 4PM: Steaming through heavy pans of ice. 12;45 pm ship stuck fast in ice. Rest of watch steaming & trying to force a passage making no progress.

Note: N end Wolstenholme Island SxW N end Saunders Island ESE1/2E.
6:30 N end Wolstenholme Island SW1/2W N end Saunders Island SxE3/4E, S end Saunders Island S1/4W.

Ice_Log: 8AM to Noon: Steaming through loose ice and short leads in a general NNE direction.  At 10:32am came to & made fast to floe.  Cast off at 11:50am and began working way through loose pans towards islands.

Note: Noon: Bearing Cary Ids. (Carey Is). 12:45 stuck in ice.

Ice_Log: 4PM to 8PM: Ice getting heavier and in larger sheets towards end of watch.
5:50pm: Made fast to heavy sheet of ice in large open water.

Note: 7:15pm: Cast off and steamed towards SE Cary Island bearing between NE & N by E dist 2 miles.

Ice_Log: 8:10pm: Came to made fast to ice floe about one mile ESE of SE point of SE island.

Note: 11:30pm: Set course for Cape Parry E by S

Ice_Log: Mid to 4AM: Steaming into Payer Harbor and at 7:30pm came to making fast to ice floe. Mid to 4AM: Steaming through leads between ice pans first hour.  cape Parry was sighted but th eice was seen to be to thick and in solid pack.  Open water to Nd & Ed.  At 3am entered perfectly open water with a few large bergs to Nd & Ed. Ships head NE.

Note: 1:20 Cape Parry SE1/2E. SE Carey Island WxS NW Carey Id NWxW1/2W. (Cape Parry should be S1/2E to make sense.)

Ice_Log: 4AM to 8AM: At close of watch no ice in sight except a small string of loose ice to Nd.
Note: 4:20 Brgs E end Hakluyt Is. SExE3/4E S end Northumberland Is. ExS1/2S Cape Parry S1/2W. (He must have meant S end Hakluyt and  E end of Northumberland)
Lying to under steam off Camp Clay.

Ice_Log: Merid to 4PM: The ice showed signs of shutting down on the shore.
4AM to 8AM: Lying fast to ice in Payer Harbor all watch. Last half of watch loose strings of heavy ice streaming to Nd along the coast.
8AM to Noon: 11am cast off from ice to make another search of Cape camp and Steamed around cape Sabine for that place.
8PM to Mid: Occasional very loose strings of heavy ice passing the island. Open water to seaward.

Mid to 4AM: Ice in vacinity in small quantity and a few bergs near the shore. Merid to 4PM: Ice in small pans but of considerable thickness. Clear water to Nd.  At 2:15pm got underway steamed out clear of theice pans and thence down the coast.
4AM to 8AM: Foulke Fjord. making fast to ice floe at mouth of fjord at 8am.
8AM to Noon: Very loose strings of ice coming down from Nd. Fast to the ice all the watch.

Note: Noon: By bearings of Foulke Fjord.

Ice_Log: 4PM to 8PM: Clear water with occasional strings of pan ice.

8PM to Mid: Steaming for Northumberland Island. At 11pm met loose pack of ice, thick but rotten and broken into very small pieces, extending several miles to Nd of Northumberland Is. Ice getting closer about 1 1/2 miles from shore.  At close of watch, forcing way slowely towards pool of water under NW corner of island, in which Thetis was made fast to large sheet.

Mid to 4AM: Steaming through loose pans of heavy ice.  At 1:40am stopped steaming. Ice moving through the narrow strait between Hakluy Id's and Northumberland Island to Sd & Ed  A large number of bergs aground to Nd & Wd of the two islands. Merid to 4PM: At 3:30 PM bar ahead showed signs of slacking, Thetis cast of and rammed at it. Cast off  at 3:50pm to help Thetis and rammed through in to narrow lead along Northumberland Island & extending towards Cape Parry.

Note: Hakluyt O brg NNW Mag 3 miles

Ice_Log: 4AM to 8AM: Lying in the ice. Hayluyt Is NNW dist about 1 league. 4AM to 8AM: Steaming in wake of Thetis. Ice in large floes with broken ice in bars in the leads between floes.  At 7:20 steamed through leads towards Cape Parry.
8AM to Noon: 9:15am got underway working yat through loose ice towards open water.  10:00am reached open water.  At 12 no leads to Sd, made fast to ice floe.

Note: Noon: By bearings Hakluyt Id (Hakluy Id's). Made fast.

Ice_Log: 8PM to Mid: At 10:40 made fast to flow. Thetis sent party ashore to leave record at Cape Parry.
Merid to 4PM: Lying in ice floe.
3am: Met loose pack of rotten ice off Wolstenholme Sound.  During rest of watch following water lanes through it for Wolstenholme Id.

4AM to 8AM: Steaming among loose ice. Passed a large glacier sparkling in the sun as far as the eye could see.  At 8 passed inside of Dalrymple Rock about one mile distant. Several small icebergs aground about the islands.
8AM to Noon: 9:45am made fast to sheet of ice between Saunders Id and Wolstenholme Id.  Open water with a few pans of rotten ice and small bergs between the two islands.
Note: Passed inside Dalrymple Rock distant 1 mile.
Brgs p.s.c. SW Head Saunders Island S3/4W, Kap Athol NWxW1/2W Wolstenholm Id. NxW1/2W

Ice_Log: 4PM to 8PM: At 5pm shifted berth and forced a ships length into fast ice to avoid the loose ice moving Sd & Ed with wind and tide. Ice very loose and rotten.  Position at 8pm Cape Athol S80W, S end of Wolstenholme Id N23E. Ships head NW by W1/2W.

8PM to Mid: Steaming towards Conical Island. Ice in large floes with open leads of water and occasional bars of broken ice .  At 9:30pm butted through large sheet breaking it up so as to allow Thetis to get - ??. Thetis made fast to large berg at 11:50.  At midnight Cape Parry bore ESE1/4E, Conical Rock W1/2S. Ships head NW by W. .

Merid to 4PM: Steaming through leads in the ice; with a few bars of sheet ice and loose ice at intervals .   Steaming geberal course along the coast ,large number of ice bergs in sight oft? end of watch, and land ahead on port bow. White mist over the sheet ice, the bergs and the land. Mid to 4AM: At 12:25am came to making fast to a berg.  At 3:20am cast off steaming into a large floe of soft ice, came to.  Conical Rock bore SSE Ships Head NW1/2N dist about 5 miles.
Note: Brgs p.s.c. Cape Pasrry ESE1/4E (should be Cape Dudley) Conical Rock WxS. 12:25 anchored.

Ice_Log: 4AM to 8AM: At 6am while forcing way through between tow large floes, Hans the Equimo jumped over the side and ran towards the glacier about two miles distant. Started with the ship full speed to head him off.  Steamed as far as we could & sent two men in chase and being fresh soon overtook him & brought him along side. durimng the chase one man fell through the ice up to his neck but with the assistance of his chum was soon out. hans could give no explination of his conduct and was palced in single iron for safe keeping.  At 8AM made fast ro a floe 1/4 mile NW side of Conical point.
Note: 4:00 came to. Conical rock SSE 5 miles. 4:50 underway.

Ice_Log: 8AM to Noon: At 9:50 cast off and followed Thetis outside Conical Rock towards cape York following edge of fast ice, about three miles off shore.
Note: 8:00 made fast NW side of Canonical Rock.

Ice_Log: 4PM to 8PM: Steaming in large lanes and through occasional ice bars.  At end of watch Bushman Id. bore on port beam, dist about 2 miles.
8PM to Mid: 9:15pm came to solid unbroken ice. Unable to see any lanes in it. Made fast to ice alongside Thetis.
Note: Bushnan Island off port bow 2 miles.

Ice_Log: Merid to 4PM: Met solid bar of heavy ice at 1PM and made fast. Mid to 4AM: No change in ice conditions.
4AM to 8AM: Both ships fast to the ice.
8AM to Noon: 9am cast off from floe steering to Sd & Wd course NW (about). .
4PM to Mid: No change in ice conditions.

Mid to 4AM: Fast to ice. Merid to 4PM: Steaming through loose ice and short leads in a general Ely direction. At 3 came to a heavy bar and began butting on port beam of Thetis. Last hour of watch engaged in ramming at bar.
4AM to 8AM: 6:30am Thetis cast off and worked to Sd, we cast of and followedher. At 8am were butting at a tight bar each ship taking turn about.
8AM to Noon: During first half of watch butting heavy pans of ice. Afterwards steaming through open leads with occasional bars of loose pans. Ice in heavy floes. .
4PM to 8PM: Broke through bar at 5pm into broad lane of clear water. Followed Thetis at full speed keeping the edge of the fast ice around the curve of Melville Bay.  At 8pm off Melville Monument.

8PM to Mid: From 8 to 10:30pm steaming forward and backward among loose pans of heavy ice meeting at last a bar of closely packed pans. Both ships backed out and at 10:30 entered a lead closer in to the coast.   At 11:35 this lead was found closed in the direction of our course. Made fast ot ice.  Bearings Ships head NW by W1/4W, Melville Monument S by E, Peaked Hill E3/4S, Cape Walker SE by S3/4S.
Merid to 4PM: Ice conditions unchanged.
3:10am: Underway following Thetis and staeming to the SD in broad water lanes.
4AM to 8AM: Butting through a light nip about 5 and since we have had a fine clear lead along floe throughout the watch.
8AM to Noon: Following edge of fast ice. At 11am met a narrow bar of heavy ice, ramming to get through it remainder of watch.
4PM to 8PM: Steaming through broad water lanes and narrow bars in SW direction.  At 7 forced way through slight bar.Thick fog coming in, coming to at 7:50.
Ice conditions unchanged. Ice slowly moving to S.W. At 2 hauled half a ship's length astern to allow "Thetis" to drop down clear of a large fle on her port bow.
At 7, the ice opening up south of us, the flagship made signal to the fleet to get underway.
The lead closing astern of the Alert, and Loch Garry not being able to work through, received verbal orders to break a way for her. Went about & broke a way for her.
Fast to the ice.

At 4:50 a bar formed by large floes blocked the passage. Thetis rammed through it & the other ships followed. Shortly afterwards a similar bar was encountered.The ships passed through except the Alert. After steaming a short distance towards open water, the Thetis followed by this ship returned and breaking up the floes made a way for the Alert.
At 7.15 entered a large lead opening into broad open water extending along the land to S'd of Horse Head.
At end of watch steaming in wake of Thetis and Loch Garry in open water & through occasional strings of rotten ice in a general W'ly (p.c.) direction.
Passed through several strings of very soft rotten ice first half. Clear water, many bergs & lumps remainder of watch.

Note: Position 1 mile  ESE True  of Berry Island

Ice_Log: At 3:30 made fast to grounded berg.
No ice in harbor and only a few bergs in sight.
A number of ice bergs in sight.
Many small bergs, no ice.
A number of large ice bergs in sight during watch.
Passing an occasional ice berg.
Numerous ice bergs in sight.
Passing occasional bergs of moderate size.
A number of ice bergs in sight.
Mid to 4AM: No ice in sight.
4AM to 8AM: Passed an ice berg close by at 5am
8PM to Mid: No ice in sight.
Mid to 4AM: Passe close by a small iceberg.
8AM to Noon: Passed several icebergs of moderate size.
4PM to 8PM: Passed several distant ice bergs during watch.
8PM-mid: Passed several icebergs during first part of watch.
Mid to 4AM: Several icebergs were sighted.
10AM: Passed large iceberg to Wd.
Mid to 4AM: Passed two ice bergs during watch.
Merid to 4PM: Several icebergs wereseen aground near coast.
4PM to 8PM: Several bergs in sight.
